title: Evol's 3D Ladders
description: Project adds New 3D Ladder Model and Texture. Includes PBR textures.
body:
![A Ladder in use next to Ocre Frog Lights and Warped Vine](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9ecd273aae2e51766661feec8fcf7b8155eaf09f.png)
# Evol's 3D Ladders for Minecraft 

Tired of flat, painted-on ladders that defy physics? **Evol's 3D Ladders** is a resource pack that replaces the vanilla Minecraft ladder with a detailed, high-quality 3D model, bringing a new level of depth and realism to your builds.

This pack is designed for everyone, from vanilla players to high-end shader enthusiasts. With Physically Based Rendering (PBR) texture maps included, ladders will react realistically to light, displaying authentic reflections and surface details when used with compatible shaders.

### Features 

* **High-Quality 3D Model:** A completely new, lovingly crafted 3D model for ladders. 
* * **PBR Textures:** Includes specular, normal, and roughness maps for stunning realism with supported shaders. 
* **Shader Ready:** Extensively tested to look great with the most popular PBR-enabled shader packs. 
* **Vanilla Friendly:** The pack still enhances ladders even without shaders, providing a better-looking model than the default. 
* **Seamless Integration:** Designed to fit perfectly with the vanilla art style.

### Installation 

1. Click the **Download** button for the latest version of the pack. 
2. Launch Minecraft with OptiFine or Iris installed. 
3. Go to **Options...** > **Resource Packs...** 
4. Click **Open Pack Folder**. 
5. Drag and drop the downloaded `.zip` file into this folder. 
6. Go back to Minecraft and select "Evol's 3D Ladders.zip" from the list of available packs to activate it. 
7. Ensure you have your favorite PBR-compatible shader pack enabled in **Options...** > **Video Settings...** > **Shaders...** to see the full effect!

### Shader Compatibility 

This resource pack's PBR features have been tested and are known to work with the following popular shader packs: * BSL Shaders * Complementary Shaders (Unbound & Reimagined) * SEUS PTGI * Sildur's Vibrant Shaders (Extreme) * Continuum 2.1
If your favorite shader supports LabPBR or OldPBR standards, it should work perfectly!
